danger_signals.visual_form: Warning light indicator on helicopter cockpit display
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy aircraft approaching from behind; immediate threat to helicopter

danger_signals.visual_form: Cockpit display showing damage condition decreasing
danger_signals.meaning: Helicopter structural integrity compromised; loss imminent if not repaired

danger_signals.visual_form: Fuel gauge on cockpit display dropping toward empty
danger_signals.meaning: Helicopter will crash if fuel reaches zero; mission failure state

danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming projectile visible on flight-path viewport
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring evasion or chaff countermeasure

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen text alert ('DEFENSE WALL' or equivalent localized text)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard approaching; collision will damage helicopter

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile figures appearing on-screen during ground infiltration segments
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat; player health depletes on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Bottom-screen health indicator during gun-shooting mode
danger_signals.meaning: Player character taking damage; life loss imminent if bar empties

danger_signals.visual_form: Ammunition counter at bottom of screen showing low rounds
danger_signals.meaning: Depleted offensive capability; inability to eliminate threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated heliport structure visible on flight-path viewport with distinct landing marker
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; helicopter repairs, refueling, and equipment change available

safety_signals.visual_form: Gun sight centered on heliport landing spot; helicopter velocity reduced to zero
safety_signals.meaning: Stable landing approach; safe descent possible

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual effect or indicator showing chaff deployment
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability to missile attacks; brief safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing or distinctly colored items appearing from weapon crates during infiltration
safety_signals.meaning: Health or ammunition restoration; increased survivability

safety_signals.visual_form: Destructible environmental object that explodes when shot
safety_signals.meaning: Eliminates all on-screen enemies simultaneously; clears immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Character model appearing in maze/infiltration segments
safety_signals.meaning: Mission-critical ally; provides intelligence and strategic information

safety_signals.visual_form: Radar display showing minimal enemy contacts relative to player position
safety_signals.meaning: Current flight corridor has reduced hostile density; relative safety

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Home heliport (allied base); allied heliports throughout mission areas marked for safe landing and resupply

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Enemy soldier uniforms and hostile military installations
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy soldier types (machine gun operators, turret positions); varying equipment and positioning strategies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy heliports (infiltration objectives); enemy base interior corridors and detention facilities; defense walls; fortified positions
Spider appears in maze/base interior segments; rescued from enemy captivity within base structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mission briefing screen with commanding officer providing tactical overview; options menu displaying equipment choices (missile types, optional gear)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player selects ordnance configuration (40 small/20 medium/10 large missiles) and chooses supplementary equipment (fuel, chaff, ground missiles) before departure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Helicopter cockpit view with gun sight overlay; immediate range radar showing approaching enemies; wide area radar indicating heliport and target positions; danger lamp illuminating when threats approach from rear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Helicopter maintains forward momentum; player must navigate between heliports while managing fuel and damage; enemies attack with missiles requiring evasion or chaff deployment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from helicopter cockpit to first-person ground view; automatically scrolling screen showing enemy soldiers and environmental hazards; health bar and ammunition counter at screen bottom
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player character advances through enemy base perimeter on foot; must eliminate all hostile soldiers appearing on-screen; collects power-ups from weapon crates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person maze exploration view showing corridor interiors; items visible in environment (rations, ammunition, vital intelligence objects); ally NPC may appear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player navigates three-dimensional base structure seeking objective items and intelligence; avoids traps; may encounter Spider providing mission-critical information

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Damage indicator on cockpit display showing degraded condition; visual effects on viewport showing helicopter strain; danger lamp frequently activating
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Helicopter responsiveness decreases; player must reach allied heliport for repairs or continue mission with reduced defensive capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fuel gauge on cockpit display approaching empty; red warning coloration on fuel indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must immediately navigate to allied heliport for refueling or helicopter will crash, ending mission

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clear screen displaying mission success message; mission helmet icon; regional variants show password (Japanese) or no password (US version)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level progression occurs; player advances to next mission with optional continue feature if lives depleted

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game over state with continue prompt; player shown number of remaining continues (up to 5 in NES version, 3 in Famicom version)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NES version forces restart of current mission; Famicom version restarts from last checkpoint; score resets to zero

environmental_storytelling.example: Helicopter dashboard displays (wide area radar, immediate range radar, fuel, damage, ammunition counters, danger lamp) communicate operational status and strategic information simultaneously; military readout aesthetic reinforces military simulation tone

Allied heliports visually distinct from enemy heliports; landing at allied bases triggers repair/refuel/equipment change sequences; landing at enemy bases forces immediate dismount and ground infiltration
Maze-mode corridor design shows detention/containment facilities where Spider is held; ration/ammunition crate placement suggests military supply infrastructure
Helicopter damage indicator provides continuous feedback on structural integrity; visual degradation signals approaching failure state without explicit text warning
Multiple enemy types (machine gunners, turret operators) with different equipment suggest escalating defensive postures within base; positioning near objectives indicates priority threat assessment
Localized on-screen text alert ('DEFENSE WALL' in US, regional equivalent in Japanese) warns of environmental hazard; placement at stage endings suggests fortified base perimeters
Ammunition and health items hidden in weapon crates; discovery requires active exploration; crate placement near high-threat areas suggests military storage protocols

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Maintains forward flight velocity; responds to pilot control input for speed adjustment, missile/cannon selection, equipment deployment
Velocity must reduce to zero; must achieve hover state; must align gun sight with heliport landing marker; must account for wind conditions during descent
Can deploy chaff grenades for brief invulnerability window; danger lamp activates as audio-visual warning; helicopter takes damage if struck directly
Approaches helicopter from rear; triggers danger lamp warning; fires missiles requiring evasion; appears on immediate range radar before visual contact

Appears on-screen during automatic scrolling segments; fires weapons at player; requires elimination via player gunfire; takes variable hits to destroy (1 HP in Famicom, 2 HP in NES version for machine gunners)
Stationary position on screen; fires projectiles at player; requires multiple hits to destroy (2 HP in Famicom, 5 HP in NES version); can be destroyed to clear threat area
Environmental destructible object; appears on screen during ground infiltration; can be shot by player
Explodes with area-of-effect damage; eliminates all on-screen enemies simultaneously; provides tactical advantage in crowded combat scenarios
Provides mission-critical intelligence and strategic information; appears in maze-mode segments; signals mission progression
Warns player with on-screen text alert; collision causes helicopter damage; indicates fortified base perimeter boundaries

notes: Laser Invasion communicates world-state primarily through dual-layer visualization: helicopter cockpit instrumentation (upper-screen tactical overlay) during flight phases provides continuous strategic feedback (radar, damage, fuel), while ground infiltration uses first-person perspective with automatic scrolling to emphasize forward momentum and time pressure. The game's three-mode structure (helicopter/gunplay/maze) transitions through visual paradigm shifts that signal narrative progression. Regional differences (Japan vs. US versions) affect difficulty through enemy HP scaling and continue mechanics, but do not substantially alter manifestation layer design. The military simulation aesthetic is consistently reinforced through technical instrumentation language, hierarchical threat visualization (danger lamp, radar proximity), and objective-based mission structure. Anti-piracy implementation (CHR bank verification) is transparent to player experience and does not affect visual communication systems documented here.
